title: Orca Bioacoustic Embeddings: site-controlled killer-whale call analysis with frozen audio-model embeddings
description: Reproducible research code for analysing killer-whale (Orcinus orca) vocalisations with frozen audio foundation-model embeddings (AVES2) on the public DCLDE 2026 corpus, with provider/site confound controls, catalogue call-type classification and cross-site transfer, first-order call-sequence structure, an animal-borne DTAG behavioural-context decode, and a representation-attribution and negative-control battery. Includes a hash-frozen embedding artifact, per-head metrics and figures, and citation-backed documentation. The work is fully non-invasive and makes no claim of semantic meaning or of a response to broadcast signals.

creators.name: Lesin, Danielle
creators.affiliation: Georgia Institute of Technology, College of Computing

notes: DCLDE 2026 killer-whale annotations are distributed by NOAA/NCEI with a public Google Cloud Storage mirror; DTAG behavioural-context recordings are openly deposited (CC-BY-4.0). Raw audio and tag archives are not redistributed here and are downloaded from their public sources by the pipeline scripts.
